assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'All 4 slices will restart simultaneously — TPU multislice maintenance is atomic, not rolling' and 'All pods across all slices terminate and recreate — there's no graceful migration for TPU multislice'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Cascading restart behavior: A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'All pods across all slices terminate and recreate' and 'Training job will be completely interrupted — expect full stop/restart cycle'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: States 'Capacity dependency risk: If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available — this could extend downtime significantly beyond 4 hours'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends 'Force a checkpoint save immediately before the maintenance window' in the checkpoint strategy section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Provides specific YAML configuration with 'RESUME_FROM_CHECKPOINT: true' environment variable and emphasizes testing resume logic throughout the response

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Provides detailed gcloud commands for applying maintenance exclusions and explains the 30-day maximum deferral period with specific use cases

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: References maintenance window coordination, checking scheduled maintenance times, and suggests scheduling during 'acceptable downtime' and 'natural pause points'
